title: Vodafone International Holdings BV v. Union of India
summary: Vodafone purchased Hutchison's Indian telecom assets through offshore acquisition of Cayman Islands holding company. Indian government sought capital gains tax on the Hutchison-Vodafone deal (~₹11,000 crore). Supreme Court ruled in Vodafone's favour — offshore transaction involving transfer of a foreign company's shares not taxable in India. Government then amended IT Act retrospectively (Finance Act 2012) to tax such deals. The retrospective amendment was later reversed by Finance Act 2021.
key_holdings: Offshore transfer of shares of foreign holding company not taxable in India under pre-2012 law
Revenue cannot look through a transaction — should look at the transaction
Tax department cannot recharacterise a legitimate commercial transaction
Every taxpayer has the right to structure affairs to minimize tax
key_quotes: The income-tax department cannot tax a non-resident on offshore transactions.
A citizen has the right to structure his affairs in a manner which would legitimately reduce the incidence of tax.
why_important: Historic tax case involving ₹11,000 crore demand. Led to Finance Act 2012 retrospective amendment (overturning the judgment), and then Finance Act 2021 reversing the retrospective amendment. Basis for India's international tax reforms.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Extremely relevant to cross-border transactions, FDI structures, and capital gains tax planning.
